home.subtitle: You are not too much. You are not the problem.

tools.breathingDesc: A 4-4-4-4 breathing cycle, with orienting if breath feels hard.
tools.groundingDesc: Use your senses to notice the room you are in.
tools.publicModeDesc: A discreet public-place reset for work, class, or family rooms.
tools.reframeDesc: Translate a painful thought into a steadier one.
tools.journalDesc: Write privately in this browser unless you choose to share or export.
tools.moodDesc: Notice state changes without turning them into verdicts.
tools.affirmDesc: A small sentence that can hold you for today.
tools.checkInDesc: Three gentle questions and one next step.

quickExit.label: Exit
quickExit.title: Quick exit and clear local data
quickExit.aria: Quick exit and clear local data

safetyGate.title: Check your safety level first.
safetyGate.body: This keeps the app from asking for reflection when your body may need help, grounding, or distance.
safetyGate.redBody: Danger, self-harm risk, coercion, severe dissociation, or trapped environment.
safetyGate.amberBody: Open the module, but begin with the grounding step. No big decisions.
safetyGate.greenBody: Continue to the selected card, script, and optional explanation.

consentGate.title: Only open this if you have choice, privacy, and permission to pause.
consentGate.body: This module is for consent, pacing, pause signals, and aftercare. It is not for graphic disclosure, pressure, persuasion, or proving anything to a partner.
consentGate.consentBody: I can stop at any time and no one is demanding access to this screen.
consentGate.unsafeBody: If someone is pressuring, watching, threatening, or blocking exit, use safety support first.
consentGate.privacyBody: Switch to discreet grounding if this screen could be seen by someone unsafe.

crisis.title: I am not safe.
crisis.body: This is for physical danger, coercion, severe dissociation, or risk of self-harm. Do not use calming tools instead of getting away, calling someone, or reaching emergency help.
steps: Move toward people, light, an exit, a lock, or a public place if you can.
Keep your phone with you. Avoid arguing through a door or in a closed room.
If you may hurt yourself, move away from blades, pills, cords, weapons, or heights.
Call one real person now. Stay connected until the danger has passed.
offlineSteps: Leave the room if you can. If leaving is not possible, lock the door or move closer to an exit, light, or another person.
Name 5 things you can see. Keep your eyes moving around the room.
Copy this message and text it to a trusted contact when you can.
crisis.trustedMessage: I am not safe. I cannot explain right now. Please call me or help me reach a safer place.
crisis.neutralScreen: If you need the screen to look neutral
crisis.openCompanion: Open companion after contacting a person

notSafe.title: If you are not safe, do not analyse this yet.
notSafe.body: This path is for physical danger, coercion, self-harm risk, severe dissociation, or being trapped somewhere unsafe. The goal is contact, distance, light, and a real human.
notSafe.lowTextBody: First: contact, distance, light, and a real human.
notSafe.offlineSubtitle: Use only the next few minutes.
notSafe.moveTitle: People, light, exit, lock, public place.
notSafe.moveBody: If you can move without increasing danger, move closer to another person, a lit area, a door, a lock, a shop, a neighbour, or the street.
notSafe.connectTitle: Call or text one real person.
notSafe.connectBody: Use plain words: “{{phrase}}” If calling is unsafe, send a short text or missed call signal.

diagnosis.infoLabel: This is information, not a diagnosis.
diagnosis.panelBody: These profiles describe protective patterns that can shift by context. Use them for self-understanding, not for diagnosing yourself or labelling someone else.
